is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria; genotyping of P. falciparum infections in Table 4.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Genotyped dhfr codons 51, 59, 108 and dhps codons 436, 437, 540 — all curated target positions (Pfdhfr:51,59,108; Pfdhps:436,437,540).

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 4 gives haplotype counts and percentages: dhfr (n=158) e.g. triple-mutant 51,59,108 CIRN 73 (46.2%); dhps (n=177) double-mutant 437,540 SGE 80 (45.2%). Allele-level frequencies extractable.

sub_country_location.evidence: All samples collected from the 'Divine Maitre' Health Centre of Shabunda town, South Kivu province, mid-east DRC — a specific sub-national site.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Collection window stated: 'Between 1 April and 13 May 2004, 435 children were screened' — single-year sampling, well within 3 years.

african_field_samples.evidence: Human clinical isolates (day 0 blood spots) from children with uncomplicated malaria in DRC, an African country.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary clinical trial with molecular genotyping of collected field samples.

duplicate_risk.evidence: MSF-run single-site trial in Shabunda; no indication samples are part of a larger consortium dataset. Cohuet et al. reported separate DRC provinces (Equator, Oriental, Katanga), not this site.

supplement.evidence: All extractable frequency data present in Table 4 of the main text; no supplementary pointer needed.

assessment.summary: Clinical trial of AS+AQ vs AS+SP in 180 children in Shabunda, South Kivu, DRC (April-May 2004). Molecular genotyping of day-0 samples gave dhfr (codons 51,59,108) and dhps (codons 436,437,540) haplotype counts and percentages (Table 4). Site-level location and single-year collection window satisfy resolution criteria. Eligible.
